2605.20385.title: ConceptSeg-R1: Segment Any Concept via Meta-Reinforcement Learning
2605.20385.paper_summary: Recent progress in promptable segmentation has shifted visual perception from object-level localization toward concept-level understanding. However, the notion of a concept remains under-specified, making it unclear whether current methods truly generalize beyond category recognition. In this work, we formalize generalized concept segmentation through a three-level taxonomy consisting of context-independent (CI), context-dependent (CD), and context-reasoning (CR) concepts, which reveals a clear capability gap across increasing levels of cognitive complexity. To address this challenge, we propose ConceptSeg-R1, a unified framework that reformulates concept segmentation as rule-induced concept grounding. At the core of our method is Meta-GRPO, a meta-reinforcement learning mechanism that learns transferable task rules from visual demonstrations and verifies them through proxy reasoning. The inferred reasoning states are then translated into segmentation-ready concept prompts via a lightweight concept translation module, enabling deductive application to target images. A shortcut routing strategy further preserves the native efficiency of segmentation models on simple cases. To systematically evaluate generalized concept segmentation, we conduct extensive experiments across diverse CI, CD, and CR concept segmentation benchmarks spanning natural, industrial, medical and reasoning-intensive domains. Without bells and whistles, ConceptSeg-R1 achieves strong performance across the full concept hierarchy while maintaining the native capability of promptable segmentation backbones. As an initial step toward segmenting any concept, we hope ConceptSeg-R1 can serve as a practical baseline for advancing segmentation from object-level prediction toward concept-level understanding.
2605.20385.paper_summary_zh: 最近在可提示分割方面的進展已將視覺感知從物體層級的定位轉向概念層級的理解。<lb/>然而，概念的概念仍然不夠明確，這使得目前的方法是否真正超越類別識別變得不清晰。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們通過一個由上下文獨立（CI）、上下文依賴（CD）和上下文推理（CR）概念組成的三層分類法，將概念分割形式化，這揭示了隨著認知複雜性增加而出現的明顯能力差距。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了ConceptSeg-R1，一個將概念分割重新定義為規則誘導的概念基礎的統一框架。<lb/>我們方法的核心是Meta-GRPO，一種元強化學習機制，從視覺示範中學習可轉移的任務規則，並通過代理推理進行驗證。<lb/>推斷出的推理狀態隨後通過輕量級概念翻譯模塊轉換為準備好分割的概念提示，從而使其能夠對目標圖像進行推理應用。<lb/>一種捷徑路由策略進一步保持了分割模型在簡單案例上的原生效率。<lb/>為了系統地評估概念分割的普遍性，我們在涵蓋自然、工業、醫療和推理密集型領域的多樣CI、CD和CR概念分割基準上進行了廣泛的實驗。<lb/>ConceptSeg-R1在整個概念層級中實現了強大的性能，同時保持了可提示分割骨幹的原生能力，沒有花哨的裝飾。<lb/>作為向任何概念分割邁出的初步步驟，我們希望ConceptSeg-R1能夠作為推進從物體層級預測到概念層級理解的實用基準。
2605.20385.author: Yuan Zhao et.al.
2605.20385.authors: Yuan Zhao, Youwei Pang, Jiaming Zuo, Wei Ji, Kailai Zhou, Bin Fan, Yunkang Cao, Lihe Zhang, Xiaofeng Liu, Huchuan Lu, Weisi Lin, Dacheng Tao, Xiaoqi Zhao
